annotations.label: Quadratic function (one variable)

anchor.snippet: a quadratic function of a single variable is a function of the form

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define a 'quadratic function' wrapper; the expression `a*(x*x)+b*x+c` appears only as an argument to `discrim` and quadratic-root lemmas.

annotations.label: Quadratic polynomial

anchor.snippet: is a quadratic polynomial , a polynomial of degree two

annotations.note: No standalone 'QuadraticPolynomial' type; polynomials of degree ≤2 are characterized via `eq_quadratic_of_degree_le_two`.

annotations.label: Quadratic equation from quadratic function

anchor.snippet: If a quadratic function is equated with zero, then the result is a quadratic equation

annotations.note: Mathlib works directly with the equation `a*(x*x)+b*x+c = 0` in `quadratic_eq_zero_iff` rather than naming the equation as a separate object.

annotations.label: Roots of a quadratic equation

anchor.snippet: The solutions of a quadratic equation are the zeros (or roots ) of the corresponding quadratic function

annotations.note: `quadratic_eq_zero_iff` characterizes the roots of `a*(x*x)+b*x+c = 0` in a field.

annotations.label: Two-variable quadratic function

anchor.snippet: a two-variable quadratic function of variables

annotations.note: Mathlib's `QuadraticMap` generalizes (homogeneous) quadratic forms but does not define an inhomogeneous bivariate quadratic polynomial function.

annotations.label: Zeros of bivariate quadratic form a conic section

anchor.snippet: In general the zeros of such a quadratic function describe a conic section

annotations.note: Conic sections are not defined in Mathlib, so this correspondence is absent.

annotations.label: Quadric

anchor.snippet: The set of its zero form a quadric

annotations.note: Quadrics as geometric zero-sets are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Etymology of 'quadratic' / square

anchor.snippet: is called a square in algebra because it is the area of a square with side

annotations.note: Etymological remark with no formal-math content to formalize.

annotations.label: Coefficients of a quadratic function

anchor.snippet: The coefficients of a quadratic function are often taken to be real or complex numbers

annotations.note: Coefficients `a b c` enter Mathlib's quadratic API as arguments to `discrim a b c`, but there is no separate 'coefficients of a quadratic function' notion.

annotations.label: Degree of a quadratic polynomial

anchor.snippet: authors sometimes mean "having degree exactly 2", and sometimes "having degree at most 2"

annotations.note: `Polynomial.degree` exists; the 'at most 2' version appears in `eq_quadratic_of_degree_le_two`, while exact-degree-2 has no dedicated name.

annotations.label: Order vs degree

anchor.snippet: more typically "order" refers to the lowest degree of a non-zero term of a power series

annotations.note: `PowerSeries.order` formalizes the order of a power series as the least nonzero-coefficient degree.

annotations.label: Univariate vs multivariate quadratic polynomial

anchor.snippet: A quadratic polynomial may involve a single variable x (the univariate case), or multiple variables

annotations.note: Mathlib distinguishes `Polynomial` (univariate) and `MvPolynomial` (multivariate) generally, not specifically for quadratics.

annotations.label: Single-variable quadratic polynomial form

anchor.snippet: Any single-variable quadratic polynomial may be written as

annotations.note: `eq_quadratic_of_degree_le_two` writes any degree-≤2 polynomial as `C a * X^2 + C b * X + C c`.

annotations.label: Two-variable quadratic polynomial form

anchor.snippet: Any quadratic polynomial with two variables may be written as

annotations.note: No normal form for bivariate degree-≤2 `MvPolynomial`s is provided in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zeros of bivariate quadratic give conic section

anchor.snippet: the zeros of a quadratic function form a (possibly degenerate) conic section

annotations.note: Conic sections are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadric surfaces/hypersurfaces

anchor.snippet: quadratic polynomials with three or more variables correspond to quadric surfaces or hypersurfaces

annotations.note: Quadric surfaces/hypersurfaces are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadratic form

anchor.snippet: Quadratic polynomials that have only terms of degree two are called quadratic forms

annotations.note: `QuadraticForm R M := QuadraticMap R M R` formalizes quadratic forms.

annotations.label: Standard form

anchor.section: Forms of a univariate quadratic function
anchor.snippet: is called the standard form

annotations.note: The `a x² + b x + c` shape appears throughout Mathlib but is not named 'standard form'.

annotations.label: Factored form

anchor.section: Forms of a univariate quadratic function
anchor.snippet: is called the factored form , where r 1 and r 2 are the roots

annotations.note: Vieta-style factorization `a*(X-r₁)*(X-r₂)` is captured by the SmallDegreeVieta lemmas, not a separate 'factored form' definition.

annotations.label: Vertex form

anchor.section: Forms of a univariate quadratic function
anchor.snippet: is called the vertex form , where h and k are the x and y coordinates of the vertex

annotations.note: No vertex-form representation is named in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graph of univariate quadratic is a parabola

anchor.snippet: the graph of a univariate quadratic function

annotations.note: Parabolas are not defined as geometric objects in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Direction of opening by sign of a

anchor.snippet: If a > 0 , the parabola opens upwards

annotations.note: Concavity-by-sign of `a` for `a x² + b x + c` is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axis of symmetry location

anchor.snippet: control the location of the axis of symmetry of the parabola

annotations.note: Axis of symmetry of a quadratic is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: y-intercept is c

anchor.snippet: it is the height of the parabola where it intercepts the y -axis

annotations.note: The y-intercept of a quadratic is not specifically formalized.

annotations.label: Vertex of a parabola

anchor.snippet: The vertex of a parabola is the place where it turns

annotations.note: Vertex of a parabola is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vertex from standard form

anchor.snippet: so the vertex, ( h , k ) , of the parabola in standard form is

annotations.note: No formula for the vertex of `a x² + b x + c` exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vertex from factored form

anchor.snippet: the average of the two roots, i.e.,

annotations.note: The 'vertex as average of roots' identity is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vertex as max or min

anchor.snippet: The vertex is also the maximum point if a < 0 , or the minimum point if a > 0

annotations.note: Mathlib relates positivity of `a x² + b x + c` to the discriminant in `discrim_le_zero`/`discrim_lt_zero` but does not state the vertex extremum result directly.

annotations.label: Axis of symmetry through vertex

anchor.snippet: that passes through the vertex is also the axis of symmetry of the parabola

annotations.note: Axis-of-symmetry-through-vertex statement is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vertex via calculus (derivative)

anchor.snippet: the vertex point, being a maximum or minimum of the function, can be obtained by finding the roots of the derivative

annotations.note: Although Mathlib has Fermat-stationary results, the specific vertex-via-derivative statement for quadratics is not stated.

annotations.label: Roots of univariate quadratic

anchor.snippet: are the values of x for which f ( x ) = 0

annotations.note: Mathlib characterizes the values `x` with `a*(x*x)+b*x+c = 0` via `quadratic_eq_zero_iff`.

annotations.label: Quadratic formula for roots

anchor.snippet: When the coefficients a , b , and c , are real or complex , the roots are

annotations.note: `quadratic_eq_zero_iff` gives `x = (-b ± s)/(2a)` where `s² = discrim a b c`, i.e., the quadratic formula.

annotations.label: Upper bound on modulus of roots

anchor.section: Upper bound on the magnitude of the roots
anchor.snippet: The modulus of the roots of a quadratic

annotations.note: No bound on `|root|` for quadratics is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square root of quadratic gives conic

anchor.section: The square root of a univariate quadratic function
anchor.snippet: The square root of a univariate quadratic function gives rise to one of the four conic sections

annotations.note: Conic sections aren't formalized, so this classification is absent.

annotations.label: Hyperbola case for square root

anchor.section: The square root of a univariate quadratic function
anchor.snippet: describes a hyperbola, as can be seen by squaring both sides

annotations.note: Hyperbolas are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ellipse/empty case for square root

anchor.section: The square root of a univariate quadratic function
anchor.snippet: describes either a circle or other ellipse or nothing at all

annotations.note: Ellipses are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Iteration of a function

anchor.snippet: one applies the function repeatedly, using the output from one iteration as the input to the next

annotations.note: `Function.iterate f n` (notation `f^[n]`) is the standard iteration of a self-map.

annotations.label: Analytically tractable iteration example

anchor.snippet: for the iterative equation

annotations.note: This specific solvable quadratic iteration example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Logistic map at r=4 (chaotic)

anchor.snippet: In the chaotic case r =4 the solution is

annotations.note: The logistic map is not formalized in Mathlib (no occurrences of 'logistic').

annotations.label: Logistic map at r=2

anchor.snippet: The solution of the logistic map when r =2 is

annotations.note: Logistic map solutions are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bivariate quadratic function

anchor.snippet: A bivariate quadratic function is a second-degree polynomial of the form

annotations.note: `QuadraticMap`/`QuadraticForm` capture the homogeneous case; the inhomogeneous bivariate polynomial function isn't separately defined.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic paraboloid case

anchor.snippet: the function has no maximum or minimum; its graph forms a hyperbolic paraboloid

annotations.note: Hyperbolic paraboloids are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elliptic paraboloid case (min/max)

anchor.snippet: the function has a minimum if both A > 0 and B > 0 , and a maximum if both A < 0 and B < 0

annotations.note: Min/max classification for bivariate quadratics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Parabolic cylinder case (no extremum)

anchor.snippet: the function has no maximum or minimum; its graph forms a parabolic cylinder

annotations.note: Parabolic cylinders are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Parabolic cylinder line of extrema

anchor.snippet: the function achieves the maximum/minimum at a line

annotations.note: Degenerate quadratic surface extremum lines are not formalized.
